topics.title: Heat disrupts U.S. holiday events
topics.summary: A major heat wave has forced changes to Fourth of July events across parts of the central and eastern U.S., including postponed parades, concerts and fireworks.
topics.discussionPrompt: Outdoor celebrations are fun, but heat can really change the mood. Would you still go to a big public event if the weather felt risky?

topics.title: Tour de France prepares for extreme heat
topics.summary: French officials have been told they can cancel Tour de France stages during red heat alerts if public safety and emergency services are at risk.
topics.discussionPrompt: It is strange to imagine canceling part of a famous race because of heat. Should big sports events become more flexible around weather?

topics.title: Markets mixed after Dow record
topics.summary: World shares were mixed after the Dow hit a new record, while some AI-related stocks recovered and others continued to struggle.
topics.discussionPrompt: AI stocks seem to swing between excitement and nerves. Do you think people are being realistic about AI, or just chasing the next big thing?

topics.title: Rare Declaration copy found in UK
topics.summary: A rare 1776 printing of the U.S. Declaration of Independence was found in the UK National Archives, attached to records from a captured American ship.
topics.discussionPrompt: It is amazing that historic papers can sit unnoticed for centuries. What forgotten object would you most like to discover in an archive?

topics.title: World Cup reaches knockout drama
topics.summary: The World Cup round of 32 continued across North America, with fans filling stadiums and the tournament moving deeper into its knockout stage.
topics.discussionPrompt: Knockout games always feel more dramatic because one mistake can end everything. Do you prefer sudden-death sports pressure or long playoff series?
